title: quercus × audleyensis
intro: Quercus × audleyensis, also known as the Audley oak, is a rare hybrid of the English oak (Quercus robur) and the sessile oak (Quercus petraea), resulting from a natural process of cross-pollination. The plant's bark, leaves, and fruits have been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Quercus × audleyensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's parts can be used to create teas, tinctures, and infusions, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
